name: Middesk Business Verification
what_it_does: Provides automated business identity verification (KYB) by checking EIN validity, secretary of state registrations, OFAC sanctions lists, business addresses, and beneficial ownership to help lenders and fintechs onboard businesses compliantly.
use_cases: Automate KYB during SMB merchant onboarding by submitting business details and retrieving verification status, registration documents, and risk signals in a single workflow
Screen businesses against OFAC and sanctions lists before extending credit or opening accounts to satisfy BSA/AML compliance requirements
Validate EIN and business address legitimacy as part of a lending underwriting pipeline before routing applications to human review
Monitor ongoing business status changes (e.g., dissolution, registration lapse) by subscribing to webhook events for portfolio risk management
Retrieve UBO (ultimate beneficial owner) data to fulfill FinCEN beneficial ownership rule requirements during account opening
not_for: Individual consumer identity verification — Middesk is purpose-built for businesses and legal entities, not natural persons
Real-time fraud scoring at transaction speed — business verification has inherent latency from government database lookups
International business verification outside the United States, where secretary of state and EIN data is not available
best_when: Best when an agent is orchestrating SMB or commercial onboarding and must satisfy KYB, AML, and beneficial ownership compliance requirements before activating an account.
avoid_when: Avoid when verifying individuals rather than businesses, or when you only need lightweight email/phone verification with no compliance obligation.

auth.notes: API key passed as Bearer token. Separate sandbox and production environments with distinct keys. Sandbox returns deterministic test data based on EIN patterns.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox environment free for development; production verifications are pay-per-use
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-verification pricing; volume discounts negotiated by contract
pricing.notes: Sandbox access is free with signup. Production requires agreement and pricing negotiation. No public pricing page.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; limits set per contract tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include structured JSON with machine-readable codes. Webhook payloads clearly indicate verification outcome and sub-reasons for failures.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Business objects have persistent IDs that can be retrieved after creation; re-submitting the same EIN does not automatically deduplicate without explicit checks.
known_agent_gotchas: Verification is asynchronous — the initial POST returns a business object in 'pending' state; agents must handle webhooks or poll the GET endpoint for completion
Secretary of state data freshness varies by state — some states update weekly, so registered status may lag real-world changes by days
OFAC screening happens automatically but match adjudication may require human review, blocking automated approval flows
Sandbox EINs must follow specific patterns documented in the sandbox guide; arbitrary test EINs will return unexpected results
Beneficial ownership data requires additional input fields not needed for basic business verification, and missing UBO data will leave those checks incomplete

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II certified. GLBA compliance maintained. PII and EIN data encrypted in transit. No public key rotation tooling documented.
